units.title: Fraction Workshop
units.description: Add, subtract, multiply, and interpret fractions.

questions.explanation: The denominators match, so add the numerators.

questions.prompt: Write 1 3/5 as an improper fraction.
Which is greater: 5/6 or 4/5?
questions.explanation: Cross products are 25 and 24, so 5/6 is greater.

questions.prompt: A trail is 3/4 mile. Lena walks it twice. How far?
A recipe uses 2/3 cup oats and 1/6 cup seeds. Total?
Noah drank 3/8 L from a 7/8 L bottle. How much remains?
Four friends share 3 pizzas equally. How much each?
A ribbon 2 1/2 m long is cut into five equal pieces. Length of each?

units.title: Decimal Lab
units.description: Calculate with decimals and powers of ten.

questions.explanation: Six tenths eight times is 48 tenths.
Move the decimal two places right.

questions.prompt: Round 18.746 to the nearest tenth.
questions.explanation: The hundredths digit is 4, so round down.

questions.prompt: A notebook costs $3.75 and a pen costs $1.40. Total?
Five apples weigh 0.24 kg each. Total mass?
A 10 m rope loses 2.65 m. How much remains?
A runner completes 2.5 km each day for 6 days. Total?
$20 is shared equally among 8 students. How much each?

units.title: Volume & Measurement
units.description: Measure volume and convert within measurement systems.

questions.prompt: Find the volume of a rectangular prism with length 6, width 4, and height 3.
questions.explanation: Volume is length times width times height.

questions.prompt: A prism has volume 96 cubic units, length 8, and width 4. Find its height.
questions.explanation: Divide 96 by the base area, 8 times 4.

questions.prompt: Convert 3.5 meters to centimeters.
Convert 2.4 liters to milliliters.
A cube has side length 5. What is its volume?
A storage box is 10 in long, 6 in wide, and 4 in high. What is its volume?
A fish tank has a base area of 180 square inches and height 12 inches. Find its volume.
questions.explanation: Volume equals base area times height.

questions.prompt: A 4.2 km trail includes a 650 m loop. How many meters in all?
questions.explanation: Convert 4.2 km to 4,200 m, then add 650.

questions.prompt: A container holds 3 L. It already has 850 mL. How many milliliters more can it hold?
questions.explanation: Convert 3 L to 3,000 mL and subtract 850.

questions.prompt: Two prisms each measure 3 by 4 by 7 units. What is their combined volume?
questions.explanation: One prism is 84 cubic units; double it.

units.title: Coordinate Pattern Quest
units.description: Plot points and analyze numerical patterns.

questions.prompt: In the point (4,7), what is the y-coordinate?
questions.explanation: The second number is the y-coordinate.

questions.prompt: Which quadrant is used in the grade 5 coordinate plane?
questions.explanation: Grade 5 works with positive x- and y-coordinates.

questions.prompt: Continue the pattern: 3, 7, 11, 15, __.
Rule A starts at 2 and adds 3. What is its fifth term?
A point moves from (2,3) to (2,8). How many units did it move?
A park map places the fountain at (3,6) and the gate at (3,1). How far apart vertically?
Pattern A adds 2 starting at 1. Pattern B adds 4 starting at 1. What is the difference between their sixth terms?
A robot starts at (1,2), moves 5 right and 3 up. Where does it stop?
questions.explanation: Add 5 to x and 3 to y.

questions.prompt: Seats are numbered 8, 13, 18, 23. What is the 10th number in the pattern?
questions.explanation: Start at 8 and add nine groups of 5.

questions.prompt: A rectangle has vertices (1,1), (6,1), (6,4), and (1,4). What is its area?
questions.explanation: Its width is 5 and height is 3.

units.title: Ratios in the Wild
units.description: Use ratios, rates, and percentages to compare quantities.

questions.prompt: Simplify the ratio 18:24.
If 5 pens cost $10, what is the unit price?
questions.explanation: Divide cost by number of pens.

questions.prompt: 30 is what percent of 120?
Complete: 4/7 = ?/21
Convert 0.65 to a percent.
A map scale is 1 cm:8 km. Two towns are 6 cm apart. Actual distance?
A class has 12 girls and 18 boys. Ratio girls:students in simplest form?
A car travels 210 miles in 3.5 hours. Average speed?
A $40 jacket is 15% off. Sale price?
Paint uses 2 parts blue for 3 parts white. For 12 cups white, blue needed?

units.title: Expression Engine
units.description: Translate, evaluate, and reason with expressions.

questions.prompt: Evaluate 4x + 3 when x = 5.
Simplify 7a + 2a.
Write an expression for 6 less than n.
Evaluate 3² + 2 × 4.
Solve x + 14 = 31.
A club charges $12 plus $4 per visit. Cost for v visits?
questions.explanation: The fixed fee is 12 and each visit adds 4.

questions.prompt: A rectangle has length x + 3 and width 5. Write its area.
Sam has twice as many cards as Mia. Together they have 36. How many does Mia have?
Tickets cost $7 each. You spend $49. How many tickets?
A tank has 80 L and drains 6 L per minute. Amount after m minutes?
questions.explanation: Start at 80 and subtract 6 each minute.

units.title: Fraction & Integer Lab
units.description: Divide fractions and reason with signed numbers.

questions.prompt: 3/4 divided by 1/8 = ?
5 divided by 2/3 = ?
What is the opposite of -14?
questions.explanation: Opposites are the same distance from zero.

questions.prompt: Order from least to greatest: -2, 4, -7.
questions.explanation: Farther left on the number line is less.

questions.prompt: Find the distance between -5 and 3.
Three quarters of a yard of ribbon is cut into pieces 1/12 yard long. How many pieces?
A 6 1/2 lb bag is split equally into 5 smaller bags. Weight of each?
The temperature falls from 6 C to -4 C. What is the change?
A diver is at -18 m and rises 7 m. What is the new position?
A recipe needs 2/5 cup oil per batch. How many batches use 3 cups?

units.title: Data & Geometry Studio
units.description: Summarize data and solve area and surface-area problems.

questions.prompt: Find the mean of 4, 7, 9, and 12.
Find the median of 2, 5, 8, 10, 13.
Find the range of 14, 9, 21, 17.
Find the area of a triangle with base 12 and height 7.
questions.explanation: Use one-half times base times height.

questions.prompt: Find the area of a parallelogram with base 9 and height 5.
questions.explanation: Area is base times perpendicular height.

questions.prompt: Five quiz scores are 70, 80, 85, 90, and 95. What is the mean?
A triangular garden has base 16 m and height 9 m. What is its area?
A rectangular prism is 2 by 3 by 5. What is its surface area?
Data values are 3, 3, 4, 5, 20. Which measure, mean or median, better represents a typical value?
questions.explanation: The outlier 20 pulls the mean upward.

questions.prompt: A room floor is 12 ft by 15 ft. Tiles cover 3 square feet each. How many tiles are needed?
questions.explanation: The area is 180 square feet; divide by 3.

units.title: Proportion Studio
units.description: Analyze proportional relationships and percent change.

questions.prompt: Find k if y = kx and y = 18 when x = 6.
Is y = 4x + 1 proportional?
questions.explanation: A proportional relationship passes through the origin.

questions.prompt: Increase 80 by 25%.
What is the slope through (0,0) and (5,15)?
Solve x/9 = 14/21.
Three concert tickets cost $82.50. What do 8 cost at the same rate?
A phone drops from $500 to $425. Percent decrease?
A recipe for 4 uses 2.5 cups flour. Flour for 10 people?
A cyclist rides 42 km in 1.5 h. Distance in 2.75 h?
Tax is 7.5% on a $64 purchase. Total?

units.title: Rational Number Quest
units.description: Operate confidently with positive and negative numbers.

questions.explanation: A negative times a negative is positive.
A positive divided by a negative is negative.

questions.prompt: The temperature is −4°C and rises 11°. New temperature?
A diver at −18 m descends 7 m. New depth?
A bank balance is −$35. A $60 deposit is made. Balance?
A team loses 3 yards on each of 5 plays. Total change?
An elevator goes from floor 6 to parking level −2. Change in floors?

units.title: Equation & Inequality Forge
units.description: Solve multi-step equations and model constraints.

questions.prompt: Solve 5x + 8 = 43.
Solve 3(x - 4) = 21.
Solve 4x - 6 = 2x + 10.
Solve x/3 + 5 = 9.
Solve the inequality 2x + 1 < 11.
A gym charges $18 plus $7 per class. Your bill is $74. How many classes?
A rectangle has perimeter 50 and width 9. Find its length.
You have $60 and spend $12 plus $6 per ticket. At most how many tickets can you buy?
questions.explanation: Solve 12 + 6t less than or equal to 60.

questions.prompt: The sum of three consecutive integers is 72. What is the middle integer?
questions.explanation: Let the integers be n-1, n, and n+1.

questions.prompt: A phone plan costs $25 plus $0.10 per text. The bill must stay under $40. What is the greatest whole number of texts?
questions.explanation: 25 + 0.10t must be less than 40, so t is less than 150.

units.title: Circles & Chance Lab
units.description: Measure circles and analyze probability and samples.

questions.prompt: Find the circumference of a circle with diameter 10 using pi = 3.14.
Find the area of a circle with radius 4 in terms of pi.
questions.explanation: Area is pi times radius squared.

questions.prompt: A fair die is rolled. Probability of rolling an even number?
questions.explanation: Three of six outcomes are even.

questions.prompt: A bag has 3 red and 7 blue marbles. Probability of red?
questions.explanation: There are 3 favorable outcomes out of 10.

questions.prompt: In 80 trials an event occurs 28 times. Experimental probability?
A circular garden has radius 6 m. Find its area in terms of pi.
A wheel has diameter 0.8 m. How far does it roll in one turn using pi = 3.14?
A spinner has 8 equal sections, 3 green. In 120 spins, about how many green results are expected?
A survey of 50 randomly selected students finds 18 prefer biking. About how many of 600 students would prefer biking?
Two fair coins are tossed. Probability of exactly one head?
questions.explanation: HT and TH are two of four equally likely outcomes.

units.title: Linear Systems Lab
units.description: Solve equations and compare linear models.

questions.prompt: Solve 3x − 7 = 20.
Find the slope through (2,5) and (6,13).
Write y = mx + b with slope −3 and intercept 4.
Solve 2(x + 5) = 26.
Solve the system y=x+2 and y=3x−4. Give x.
Plan A costs $15 + $8/month; Plan B costs $35 + $4/month. After how many months are costs equal?
A taxi charges $3.50 plus $2.25 per mile. Cost for 12 miles?
A tank starts with 120 L and loses 8 L/min. When will 40 L remain?
Two numbers total 38 and differ by 6. What is the larger number?
A line passes through (0,−2) and (7,12). Write its equation.

units.title: Geometry in Motion
units.description: Reason with transformations, similarity, and volume.

questions.prompt: Reflect (4,−3) across the x-axis.
questions.explanation: The x-coordinate stays; y changes sign.

questions.prompt: Rotate (2,5) 90° counterclockwise about the origin.
A triangle dilates by scale factor 3. A side of 4 becomes?
Find the hypotenuse when legs are 6 and 8.
Volume of a cylinder with r=3, h=5 in terms of π?
A 15-ft ladder reaches 12 ft up a wall. How far is its base from the wall?
A model uses scale 1:50. A 4 cm wall represents how many cm?
A cone has r=3, h=8. Volume in terms of π?
A square of side 5 is dilated by 1.6. New area?
Points A(1,2) and B(7,10). What is AB?

units.title: Function Design Lab
units.description: Compare functions across equations, tables, and contexts.

questions.prompt: If f(x) = 3x - 2, find f(5).
Is the relation {(1,4),(2,5),(1,7)} a function?
A function has values (0,6), (1,10), (2,14). What is its rate of change?
questions.explanation: The output increases by 4 when input increases by 1.

questions.prompt: Write a rule for outputs 5, 8, 11, 14 when inputs are 0, 1, 2, 3.
questions.explanation: The rate is 3 and initial value is 5.

questions.prompt: Which grows faster: y=2x+9 or y=3x-4?
A candle is 24 cm tall and burns 1.5 cm per hour. Write its height after h hours.
questions.explanation: Start at 24 and subtract 1.5 each hour.

questions.prompt: A streaming plan costs $8 plus $2 per movie. Find the cost of 7 movies.
Machine A makes 45 parts initially and 12 per hour. Machine B makes 21 initially and 16 per hour. When are totals equal?
A line has y-intercept -3 and passes through (4,9). What is its slope?
questions.explanation: Rise from -3 to 9 is 12 over a run of 4.

questions.prompt: A tank contains 90 L and drains linearly. After 5 minutes it has 65 L. What is the rate of change?

units.title: Exponents & Data Signals
units.description: Use scientific notation and interpret bivariate data.

questions.prompt: Simplify 2^3 times 2^4.
questions.explanation: Add exponents with the same base.

questions.prompt: Write 0.00056 in scientific notation.
questions.explanation: Move the decimal four places right.

questions.prompt: Write 7.2 x 10^5 in standard notation.
questions.explanation: Move the decimal five places right.

questions.prompt: Find the square root of 196.
A scatter plot rises from left to right. What type of association?
questions.explanation: As x increases, y tends to increase.

questions.prompt: Earth is about 1.5 x 10^8 km from the Sun. Mars is about 2.28 x 10^8 km away. Difference in scientific notation?
questions.explanation: Subtract to get 0.78 x 10^8, then rewrite.

questions.prompt: A bacteria count triples each hour from 200. Write the count after h hours.
questions.explanation: The initial value is 200 and growth factor is 3.

questions.prompt: A square has area 288 square units. What is its side length in simplest radical form?
questions.explanation: The square root of 288 is 12 times square root 2.

questions.prompt: A two-way table shows 30 of 50 athletes and 20 of 50 non-athletes prefer mornings. Which group has the greater morning preference rate?
A trend line predicts y=4.5x+12. Predict y when x=20.

references.label: Illustrative Mathematics Tasks
references.note: Open task structures and mathematical practices

references.label: Open Up Resources 6–8 Math
references.note: CC BY curriculum sequence and lesson contexts

references.label: California ELA Language Standards
references.note: Context clues, roots, reference skills, and word precision

references.label: IM Grade 7 Course Guide
references.note: Open unit sequence for algebra, geometry, and probability
